Description: A mild viral infection of the nose and throat. Very common and gets better in a week.
A viral infection that attacks your respiratory system. Much stronger than a common cold.
An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid.
A serious bacterial infection that mainly affects the lungs. Spreads through air.
A condition that makes airways narrow and swell, causing breathing difficulty especially at night.
Inflammation of the bronchial tubes that carry air to your lungs.
A viral disease caused by coronavirus. Ranges from mild to very serious.
Inflammation of the sinuses, the hollow spaces around your nose.
Inflammation of the lining around the lungs that causes sharp chest pain when breathing.
Stomach infection causing vomiting and loose motions. Usually lasts 1-3 days.
Illness caused by eating contaminated food. Symptoms start within hours of eating bad food.
Stomach acid frequently flows back into your food pipe, causing burning feeling in chest.
Painful sores that develop on the inside lining of your stomach.
Inflammation of the appendix. Pain usually starts around navel and moves to lower right side.
A common disorder affecting the large intestine causing cramps, bloating and changed bowel habits.
A serious bacterial infection causing severe watery diarrhoea and dehydration. Can be fatal without treatment.
Difficulty passing stools or not passing stools frequently enough.
Yellowing of skin and eyes caused by too much bilirubin in blood. Often a sign of liver problem.
Liver infection caused by Hepatitis A virus, usually spread through contaminated food or water.
Serious liver infection caused by Hepatitis B virus. Can become chronic.
A viral infection causing liver inflammation, sometimes leading to serious liver damage.
Late-stage liver disease where healthy liver tissue is replaced by scar tissue.
Infection in any part of the urinary system. More common in women.
Hard deposits of minerals and salts that form inside kidneys. Cause severe pain when passing.
Gradual loss of kidney function over time. Kidneys filter waste from blood.
Kidney infection usually caused by bacteria. Needs immediate antibiotic treatment.
A blockage that stops blood flow to the heart. Life threatening emergency.
High blood pressure. Known as a silent killer because it usually has no symptoms until severe.
Chest pain caused by reduced blood flow to the heart muscle. Usually triggered by activity.
The heart is not pumping blood as well as it should. Symptoms worsen over time.
An irregular heartbeat — heart beats too fast, too slow, or with an irregular pattern.
Intense throbbing headache, usually on one side. Can last hours or days.
Brain attack — blood supply to part of brain is cut off. Time is critical. FAST: Face, Arms, Speech, Time.
A brain disorder causing repeated seizures or fits. Can be controlled with medicines.
Inflammation of membranes surrounding brain and spinal cord. Medical emergency.
Feeling that you or surroundings are spinning or moving when standing still.
A progressive nervous system disorder that affects movement. Tremors are a common sign.
A condition where blood sugar stays too high. Can damage kidneys, eyes, heart and nerves.
Low blood sugar. Happens quickly and needs immediate eating of sugar or sweets.
Thyroid gland not producing enough hormones. Slows down body functions.
Thyroid gland produces too much hormone, speeding up body functions.
Excess body fat that increases risk of many diseases including diabetes and heart disease.
A mosquito-borne disease caused by parasites. Fever comes in cycles with chills and sweating.
A mosquito-borne viral disease. Causes severe flu-like illness. Watch for bleeding signs.
A bacterial infection spread through contaminated food and water. Fever slowly rises over days.
A highly contagious viral infection causing itchy blister-like rash all over body.
A highly contagious viral disease. Rash starts on face and spreads down. Preventable by vaccine.
A mosquito-borne viral disease. Joint pain can be severe and last months.
Bacterial infection spread through water contaminated by animal urine. Common after floods.
A deadly viral disease from animal bites. Must be treated immediately after bite.
Infection caused by fungi on skin. Very common in warm, moist areas of body.
A skin disease causing red, itchy, scaly patches. Chronic condition with flares and remissions.
A condition making skin inflamed, itchy and rough. Very common in children.
A skin condition that occurs when hair follicles become plugged with oil and dead skin cells.
Red, itchy welts on skin caused by allergic reaction. Can appear anywhere on body.
A highly contagious bacterial skin infection causing sores and yellow crusts. Common in children.
Inflammation of joints causing pain and stiffness. Gets worse with age.
An autoimmune disease where immune system attacks joints. Causes pain, swelling and eventually joint damage.
Bones become weak and brittle, making fractures more likely. Common in older women.
Form of arthritis with sudden severe pain, usually in big toe. Caused by excess uric acid.
Pain in the lower back. One of the most common reasons people visit doctors.
Age-related wear and tear of neck bones and discs. Very common after age 40.
Not enough healthy red blood cells to carry oxygen. Causes tiredness and weakness.
A genetic blood disorder where red blood cells become abnormal shaped causing blockages.
Severe form of dengue. Bleeding from multiple sites is a warning sign of serious danger.
A mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness and loss of interest. Very treatable.
Feeling of worry, nervousness or fear that is overwhelming and interferes with daily life.
Inflammation of the tonsils at the back of the throat. Common in children.
Middle ear infection. Very common in young children. Causes severe ear pain.
Inflammation of the eye surface. Called pink eye. Highly contagious.
Polycystic Ovary Syndrome — a hormonal disorder common among women of reproductive age.
Tissue similar to uterus lining grows outside uterus. Causes severe pain during periods.
Cancer that begins in the lungs. Strongly linked to smoking. Early detection is key.
Cancer that begins in the stomach. Often found late because early symptoms are vague.
An allergic reaction causing cold-like symptoms. Triggered by pollen, dust or animal dander.
An abnormal reaction of the immune system to a medicine.
A severe, life-threatening allergic reaction. Usually triggered by food, medicines or insect stings.
Your body loses more fluids than it takes in. Dangerous if severe, especially in children.
A life-threatening condition where body overheats above 40°C. Medical emergency.',
Not getting enough nutrients from food. Very common in poor communities. Treatable with proper food.',
Intestinal worms living inside the gut. Very common in children in rural areas.',
A contagious skin infestation by tiny mites causing intense itching especially at night.
Swollen veins in the anus and rectum. Cause pain and bleeding during bowel movements.
